kalshi_title: Fed cuts rates by 25bps at Dec 2026 meeting
polymarket_question: Will 4 Fed rate cuts happen in 2026?
hypothesis: Polymarket is annual-cumulative count (full year 2026); Kalshi is a single meeting. Classic scope trap — a naive title matcher sees 'Fed / cut / 2026' on both. Poly counts emergency cuts and 50bps as 2; Kalshi counts only scheduled 25bps as 25bps.
label: UNLABELED

kalshi_title: Fed cuts rates by 25bps at Jun 2026 meeting
polymarket_question: Will 4 Fed rate cuts happen in 2026?
hypothesis: Same Polymarket as pair-0001 paired against a different single meeting — useful to confirm the scope label is stable across which meeting we pick.
label: UNLABELED

kalshi_title: Fed cuts rates by 25bps at Dec 2026 meeting
polymarket_question: Will 3 Fed rate cuts happen in 2026?
hypothesis: Same structure as pair-0001 but a different cumulative threshold (3 vs 4). Confirms the scope-trap label doesn't depend on the specific cut count Polymarket asks about.

kalshi_title: Fed cuts rates by >25bps at Jul 2026 meeting
polymarket_question: Will 7 Fed rate cuts happen in 2026?
hypothesis: Scope trap with an added twist — a >25bps cut counts as 2+ cuts in Polymarket's accounting. Tests whether the scope label still applies when the per-meeting outcome has a non-1 contribution to the cumulative count.
label: UNLABELED

kalshi_title: Fed cuts rates by 25bps at Apr 2026 meeting
polymarket_question: Will the Fed Cut-Pause-Pause in the next three decisions (Apr-Jun-Jul)?
hypothesis: Polymarket is a conjunction over three specific FOMC meetings (a sequence). Kalshi is the cut leg of the first meeting only. Tests whether the engine recognizes one-leg-of-sequence as a distinct relationship from equivalence.
label: UNLABELED

kalshi_title: Fed maintains rate at Jun 2026 meeting
polymarket_question: Will the Fed Cut-Pause-Pause in the next three decisions (Apr-Jun-Jul)?

polymarket_question: Will the Fed Cut-Pause-Pause in the next three decisions (Apr-Jun-Jul)?
hypothesis: Kalshi is the 'opposite' of the Polymarket first leg — if Kalshi YES then Polymarket must be NO. Tests whether the engine picks up negatively-correlated one-leg relationships.

hypothesis: Titles look similar (both mention Fed cuts + a year) but the years differ (Kalshi 2027 vs Polymarket 2026). Classic hard negative for embedding-based retrievers.

polymarket_question: Will the Fed Cut-Pause-Pause in the next three decisions (Apr-Jun-Jul)?
hypothesis: Kalshi is for Apr 2027; Polymarket's 'next three decisions' (as of early 2026) are Apr/Jun/Jul 2026. Month matches but year doesn't. Harder because Poly doesn't explicitly say '2026' in the title.

kalshi_title: Fed hikes rates by 25bps at Jun 2026 meeting
polymarket_question: Fed Rate Hike by June 2026 Meeting?
hypothesis: Polymarket asks about ANY hike in the window Dec 2025 - end of Jun 2026 meeting. Kalshi asks specifically about a 25bps hike at the Jun 2026 meeting. Kalshi YES implies Polymarket YES, but not vice versa (could hike 50bps, or hike at an earlier meeting).

kalshi_title: Fed hikes rates by 25bps at Apr 2026 meeting
polymarket_question: Fed Rate Hike by June 2026 Meeting?

kalshi_title: Fed hikes rates by 25bps at Jul 2026 meeting
polymarket_question: Fed Rate Hike by June 2026 Meeting?
hypothesis: Kalshi is a meeting AFTER Polymarket's window (Poly window ends at end of Jun 2026 meeting). A 25bps hike in July cannot affect Polymarket's resolution. Near-miss on date — tests whether the engine correctly treats 'after the cutoff' as unrelated.
label: UNLABELED

kalshi_title: Fed upper bound above 1.50% after Jan 2027 meeting
polymarket_question: Will the Fed's lower bound reach 1.25% or lower before 2027?
hypothesis: Scalar-threshold trap: Polymarket 'lower bound <= 1.25%' is algebraically equivalent to 'upper bound <= 1.50%'. Kalshi asks upper > 1.50% — the OPPOSITE direction. Plus Kalshi is POINT (at Jan 2027) vs Poly INTERVAL (ever before 2027). Related but not equivalent.
label: UNLABELED

kalshi_title: Fed upper bound above 1.25% after Jan 2027 meeting
polymarket_question: Will the Fed's lower bound reach 1.25% or lower before 2027?
hypothesis: Scalar-threshold near-miss: both mention '1.25%' but they mean very different things. Polymarket is lower bound; Kalshi is upper bound. Same number, ~25bps apart in meaning. Hard for a title embedding.
label: UNLABELED

kalshi_title: Fed upper bound above 1.00% after Jan 2027 meeting
polymarket_question: Will the Fed's lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
hypothesis: Polymarket lower <= 0.5% equals upper <= 0.75%. Kalshi asks upper > 1.00%. If Kalshi YES at Jan 2027, upper > 1.00% at that point — but Polymarket could still have been YES earlier. Logically overlapping scenarios but not equivalent.
label: UNLABELED

kalshi_title: Fed upper bound above 3.00% after Jan 2027 meeting
polymarket_question: Will the Fed's lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
hypothesis: Polymarket asks about lower bound reaching 0.5% (very low-rate regime). Kalshi asks about upper > 3.00% (moderate-rate regime). The strikes are far apart on the rate axis — should be much easier to flag as unrelated than pair-0017.
label: UNLABELED

polymarket_question: Will 4 Fed rate cuts happen in 2026?

kalshi_title: Fed maintains rate at Dec 2026 meeting
polymarket_question: Will 4 Fed rate cuts happen in 2026?
hypothesis: Scope trap where the Kalshi outcome is 'no cut' (hold). Contributes 0 to Polymarket's cumulative count. Tests that the label reflects STRUCTURAL relationship not outcome correlation.

kalshi_title: Fed upper bound above 1.50% after Jan 2027 meeting
polymarket_question: Will the Fed's lower bound reach 1.25% or lower before 2027?
hypothesis: Polymarket counts rate changes at ANY point (including emergency/unscheduled actions); Kalshi only reflects the rate level at the scheduled Jan 2027 meeting. Even setting aside the point-vs-interval mismatch, the 'includes emergencies' asymmetry is a distinct trap.
